name: Grilled Beef Tenderloin with Red Wine and Pistachios
recipeIngredient: 2 cups dry red wine, preferably Pinot Noir
1 dash fresh ground black pepper to taste
2 pounds beef tenderloin, cut into 8-oz steaks
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the grill or broiler.
STEP ONE: Making the Red Wine Sauce--
In a large saucepan, combine the stock, red wine, roasted garlic, shallots, and chopped parsley. Bring to a simmer over medium heat and cook until reduced to coat the back of a spoon, about 20 minutes. Transfer to a blender and puree until smooth. Strain through a fine sieve into another saucepan, then adjust the salt and pepper. Stir in the remaining parsley.
STEP TWO:
In a small bowl, combine the remaining garlic, pistachios, sunflower seeds, and Red Wine Sauce. Mix well. Rub the surface of the steaks with oil.
STEP THREE: Grilling the Steaks--
Grill until well-seared on the surface, about 5 minutes. Turn over and cook until you reach desired doneness, about 4 minutes for medium-rare, depending on thickness.
STEP FOUR:
Brush the tops of the steaks with a small amount of Red Wine Sauce, then press the steaks into the pistachio mixture, coating the surface well. Position the steaks on serving plates, spoon the remaining sauce around them, garnish with parsley sprigs, and serve.
